data.title: Top Brass Cash Out: Major Insider Stock Sales Rock Applied Digital
data.description:
New insider activity at Applied Digital Corporation ( (APLD) ) has taken place on January 16, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Recent insider activity at Applied Digital Corporation shows notable share sales by top executives. Director Richard Nottenburg disposed of 23,606 shares of company stock in a transaction totaling $834,859. In a separate, larger move, CEO and Chairman Wes Cummins sold 165,000 shares of Applied Digital Corporation, generating proceeds of $6,009,300. These transactions highlight significant insider selling at the company, with combined stock sales exceeding 188,000 shares and total value surpassing $6.8 million.

Recent Updates on APLD stock

Over the last day, Applied Digital’s stock has seen notable volatility, with shares recently trading in the mid-$30s and pulling back around 4% in the context of heavy but mixed options activity: call volume continues to outpace puts, yet put–call skew has steepened as traders seek more downside protection and implied volatility, though elevated, sits in the lower end of its one-year range. Fundamentally, sentiment is being shaped by the company’s ongoing AI infrastructure expansion, highlighted by its Polaris Forge AI data center campuses and recent large hyperscaler lease wins, which give analysts greater visibility into near-term revenue ramps and underpin constructive long-term views. At the same time, Spark’s Neutral stance reflects concern that, despite the strong technical uptrend and upbeat earnings-call commentary, Applied Digital still exhibits weak financial quality—deep losses, negative operating and free cash flow, and high leverage—while its valuation is harder to anchor due to negative earnings and the absence of dividend support. On the corporate side, the board’s move to elevate co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer Jason Zhang to President, while maintaining Wes Cummins as CEO, is viewed as reinforcing strategic continuity and execution around the AI build-out rather than signaling a change in direction, contributing to a generally positive but risk-aware outlook behind recent analyst reassessments of the stock’s potential.

Spark’s Take on APLD Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, APLD is a Neutral.

The score reflects a strong technical uptrend and a notably positive earnings-call backdrop driven by large hyperscaler lease wins and a visible near-term ramp, but is held back by weak financial quality—deep losses, negative operating cash flow, very large negative free cash flow, and elevated leverage. Valuation is also constrained by negative earnings (negative P/E) and no stated dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on APLD stock, click here.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Top Unity Bancorp Insiders Quietly Cash Out in a Series of Stock Sales
data.description:
New insider activity at Unity Bancorp ( (UNTY) ) has taken place on January 16, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Unity Bancorp has seen notable insider selling activity, with three senior executives disposing of shares in recent transactions. Director of Mortgage Lending/FSVP Vincent Geraci sold 1,573 shares of Unity Bancorp stock for a total of $87,112. Chief Lending Officer/FSVP James Joseph Donovan sold 777 shares, generating proceeds of $43,566. In addition, Chief Credit Officer/SVP Minsu Kim sold 750 shares, with the transaction valued at $42,097. These combined insider sales highlight significant share disposals by key members of Unity Bancorp’s leadership team.

Recent Updates on UNTY stock

In the past 24 hours, Unity Bancorp has drawn positive analyst attention following strong quarterly and full-year results, which in turn have supported higher valuation expectations for the stock. Piper Sandler and Keefe Bruyette each raised their price targets after Unity reported a solid quarter featuring a small beat on net interest income, core return on assets above 2%, resilient loan yields, and a stable net interest margin. Analysts highlighted Unity’s strong capital position, robust loan growth momentum—particularly in commercial portfolios—and healthy deposit growth as positioning the bank well for a more growth-oriented environment. Management also reported record annual net income with materially higher ROA and ROE year over year, and noted continued execution on its organic growth strategy plus an unrealized gain tied to the resolution of Patriot National Bancorp, reinforcing the perception of Unity as a high-performing institution and providing the key rationale behind the recent upward revisions to price targets.

Spark’s Take on UNTY Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, UNTY is a Outperform.

Unity Bancorp’s strong financial performance and attractive valuation are the primary drivers of its high score. The technical indicators suggest a positive trend, although caution is advised due to potential overbought conditions. Recent corporate events further bolster investor confidence.

data.title: HEICO (HEI) Shines With Record Earnings and Growth
data.description:
Heico Cp Cl (($HEI.A)) has held its Q4 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

HEICO’s latest earnings call painted a decidedly upbeat picture, with management emphasizing record profitability, broad-based revenue growth, and strong cash generation. While executives acknowledged several macro and industry-specific risks, the tone remained confident, supported by robust operational performance and a disciplined acquisition strategy that is driving scale without overleveraging the balance sheet.

Record Net Income Underscores Strong Bottom-Line Momentum

Consolidated net income jumped 35% to a record $188.3 million, or $1.33 per diluted share, in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, up from $139.7 million, or $0.99 per diluted share, a year earlier. This surge in earnings reflects a combination of higher sales across the business, improved operating performance, and effective cost management. For investors, such a sharp increase in net income signals that HEICO is translating top-line strength into tangible shareholder value.

Flight Support Group Delivers Standout Top- and Bottom-Line Gains

The Flight Support Group was a key engine of growth, with net sales rising 21% to a record $834.4 million in Q4 2025, compared with $691.8 million in the prior-year quarter. Operating income advanced even faster, up 30%, underscoring operating leverage as volumes scale. This segment’s performance suggests healthy demand for aftermarket aviation products and services, as well as effective execution in pricing and cost control. For market watchers, the Flight Support Group’s record results highlight HEICO’s strong positioning in commercial and defense-related aviation support.

Electronic Technologies Group Grows, but Margins Tighten Slightly

HEICO’s Electronic Technologies Group also turned in record results, with net sales climbing 14% to $384.8 million in Q4 2025 from $336.2 million a year earlier. Operating income increased 10%, indicating solid profitability even as the business scaled. However, the operating margin edged down from 24.3% in Q4 2024 to 23.3% in Q4 2025. Management’s comments suggest that while demand remains strong, the group is seeing a mix of factors—such as product mix and cost pressures—that are modestly weighing on margins, a point investors will likely watch in coming quarters.

EBITDA Growth and Deleveraging Strengthen Financial Profile

Consolidated EBITDA grew 26% to $331.4 million in Q4 2025, compared with $264 million in Q4 2024, underscoring the company’s ability to generate strong earnings before non-cash and financing-related items. At the same time, HEICO improved its net debt-to-EBITDA ratio to 1.60, signaling a healthier leverage position despite ongoing acquisition activity. This combination of higher EBITDA and lower leverage enhances financial flexibility, giving HEICO room to continue investing for growth while maintaining balance sheet discipline.

Robust Cash Flow Fuels Investment Capacity

Cash flow from operating activities surged 44% to $295.3 million in Q4 2025, up from $205.6 million a year earlier. Such strong cash generation provides the company with ample capacity to fund acquisitions, capital expenditures, and potential shareholder-friendly actions without relying excessively on external financing. For investors, the sharp increase in operating cash flow is a critical confirmation that earnings quality is high and that the business model is converting profits into cash efficiently.

Acquisition Strategy Continues to Drive Scale and Diversification

HEICO completed five acquisitions during fiscal 2025, each contributing to higher sales, earnings, and cash flow. Management emphasized that these deals are performing accretively and that the company expects additional acquisitions in fiscal 2026. The ongoing acquisition strategy remains central to HEICO’s growth playbook, enabling it to expand product lines, deepen customer relationships, and gain niche technologies. This roll-up approach, backed by strong cash flow and manageable leverage, is a key reason why the company’s growth profile remains compelling.

Market and Policy Headwinds Remain on the Radar

Despite the strong results, management highlighted several potential headwinds that could impact future performance. These include the risk of lower commercial air travel, changes in airline fleet composition, and potential reductions in defense spending. Each of these factors could dampen demand in parts of HEICO’s portfolio. While the company’s diversified business model and acquisition strategy provide some cushion, investors should be aware that macro trends in aviation and defense markets remain an important swing factor.

Higher SG&A Reflects Investment and Compensation Costs

The company reported higher selling, general and administrative (SG&A) expenses, primarily driven by increased share-based compensation. While this adds to operating costs, management framed it as part of a broader effort to retain and incentivize talent, which is critical for sustaining innovation and long-term growth. For shareholders, the key question will be whether these incremental costs continue to be offset by the strong revenue and earnings growth HEICO is generating.

Forward-Looking Outlook Points to Continued Growth

Looking ahead, HEICO signaled confidence in its ability to sustain growth in net sales, supported by both organic demand and the contributions of recent acquisitions. Management expects continued strength in its core segments and reiterated its focus on strategic, earnings-accretive acquisitions to further enhance shareholder value. Record net income, record operating income, and record net sales set a high bar, but the company’s improved leverage profile and strong cash flow give it a solid foundation to pursue its growth agenda in fiscal 2026 and beyond.

HEICO’s latest earnings call reinforced the narrative of a company executing well on both operations and strategy. With record profits, strong segment performance, rising EBITDA, and powerful cash generation, the overall sentiment was distinctly positive, even as management acknowledged external risks and modest margin pressures in parts of the business. For investors, the key takeaway is that HEICO appears well-positioned to continue its growth trajectory while maintaining financial discipline and pursuing targeted acquisitions in the year ahead.

data.title: Wipro Earnings Call: Margin Strength, Growth Still Muted
data.description:
Wipro Limited ((WIT)) has held its Q3 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wipro Balances Margin Strength With Muted Growth Outlook in Latest Earnings Call

Management at Wipro Limited struck a tone of cautious optimism in its latest earnings call, highlighting notable progress on margins, cash generation, and AI-led capability expansion while acknowledging continuing demand softness and execution timing issues. The quarter showed that operational discipline and a strong balance sheet are offsetting weak year-on-year revenue trends, sector-specific pressures, and delayed ramp-ups in key deals, leaving near-term growth visibility modest but not deteriorating.

Sequential Revenue Growth Supported by Scale, But Organic Momentum Still Soft

Wipro’s IT Services revenue reached $2.64 billion, rising 1.4% sequentially in constant currency (1.2% reported). However, underlying organic growth remains subdued: excluding the recently acquired HARMAN DTS, sequential constant-currency growth was only 0.6%. On a year-on-year basis, IT Services revenue slipped 1.2% in constant currency (though reported revenue was marginally positive at +0.2%), reflecting a demand environment that is still cautious and marked by delays in customer decision-making and ramp-ups.

Margin Expansion Delivers One of Wipro’s Best Performances in Recent Quarters

Profitability stood out as a key positive. Operating margin improved to 17.6%, up 40 basis points versus the adjusted prior quarter and 10 basis points year-on-year. Management described this as one of the strongest margin prints in recent quarters, driven by cost discipline and operational efficiency. Importantly for investors, Wipro signaled its intent to maintain margins in a broadly similar band of around 17%–17.5%, even as it absorbs acquisition-related dilution and continues to invest in growth areas such as AI and engineering.

Cash Generating Machine With a $6.5 Billion War Chest

Cash generation remained robust, underscoring the company’s financial resilience. Operating cash flow came in at 135% of net income for the quarter, a strong conversion that reinforces confidence in earnings quality. Wipro ended the period with gross cash and investments of $6.5 billion, giving it substantial flexibility to fund acquisitions, internal investments, and shareholder returns. This balance sheet strength provides a significant cushion against near-term macro and demand uncertainties.

Healthy Deal Wins and Contract Value Despite Timing Noise

Deal activity stayed solid, even if some ramp-ups are taking longer than expected. Wipro reported total contract value (TCV) of $3.3 billion, including $871 million in large deal bookings. While these figures reflect continued success in winning meaningful engagements, management emphasized that large deal bookings can be volatile from quarter to quarter and that the current period included some delays and timing-related lumpiness rather than any structural weakening in competitiveness.

HARMAN DTS Acquisition Bolsters Engineering and AI Capabilities

The completed acquisition of HARMAN DTS made an immediate contribution, adding about 0.8% to constant-currency revenue in the quarter. Strategically, the deal significantly strengthens Wipro’s engineering and AI capabilities, particularly within its engineering global business line and product innovation portfolio. Management positioned this acquisition as central to gaining greater relevance in high-value engineering, digital products, and AI-led services, even though they cautioned that it will initially dilute margins.

AI-First Strategy Gains Traction With New Platforms and Labs

Wipro doubled down on its AI-first narrative, rolling out the Wipro Intelligence suite across both industry and delivery platforms. New offerings such as PayerAI, NetOxygen, and AutoCortex target vertical-specific use cases, while delivery platforms like WINGS and Vega aim to embed AI in execution and productivity. The company is also expanding its network of innovation labs in the U.S., Australia, and the Middle East to accelerate AI-led transformation for clients, positioning itself to capture the next wave of digital spending once demand normalizes.

Steady Earnings and Improved Tax Efficiency

On the bottom line, adjusted net income reached INR 33.6 billion, with adjusted EPS at INR 3.21—up 3.5% sequentially and flat year-on-year. While top-line growth has been muted, the company’s ability to expand margins and manage its tax profile has supported earnings stability. The effective tax rate improved to 23.9%, down from 24.4% a year earlier, providing a modest tailwind to net income even in a sluggish revenue environment.

Shareholder Returns Remain a Priority

Wipro continued to return capital aggressively to investors. The board declared an interim dividend of INR 6 per share, and total cash returned to shareholders so far this financial year has exceeded $1.3 billion. Management reiterated that share buybacks remain on the table as a capital allocation option, signaling a willingness to deploy its substantial cash reserves in ways that can support shareholder value if growth remains constrained.

Year-on-Year Revenue Decline Highlights Demand Challenges

Despite the sequential uptick, the year-on-year revenue decline of 1.2% in constant currency highlights the demand and execution challenges the company still faces. Management acknowledged subdued organic growth and only modest visibility for the near term, as some clients continue to prioritize cost takeout and vendor consolidation over new discretionary projects. This backdrop tempers the otherwise positive narrative around margin and cash performance.

Soft Guidance Underscores Near-Term Growth Headwinds

Management’s outlook for the coming quarter was cautious. Wipro guided Q4 IT Services revenue to $2.635–$2.688 billion, implying 0%–2% sequential constant-currency growth, with the range including two months of HARMAN DTS revenue. The muted guidance reflects fewer working days in the quarter and delays in ramp-ups of certain large deals, which are pushing out the full impact of earlier wins. While the pipeline remains healthy, these timing issues and macro caution are constraining near-term growth.

Sector Weakness in Energy, Manufacturing & Resources

The EMR (Energy, Manufacturing & Resources) vertical was a notable weak spot. Revenue declined 4.9% sequentially and 5.8% year-on-year, with management citing tariff uncertainty and ongoing supply-chain disruptions as key headwinds. Despite these declines, Wipro pointed to a healthy pipeline in EMR, particularly around vendor consolidation and cost optimization opportunities, suggesting potential for recovery once macro conditions stabilize.

Consumer Vertical Under Pressure Despite Sequential Uptick

The Consumer segment delivered a modest 0.7% sequential revenue increase but remained down 5.7% year-on-year. The decline was driven by tariff-related pressures and the suspension of a large SAP program by a key customer. This underscores how client-specific project decisions and macro sensitivities are weighing on growth, even in areas where Wipro continues to see underlying demand for digital transformation.

Americas 2 and Capco Face Furloughs and Regional Weakness

In geographic terms, the Americas 2 region continued to drag on performance, with revenue declining 0.8% sequentially and 5.2% year-on-year. Within this, Capco—a key acquisition focused on consulting in financial services—was impacted by client furloughs and ended the quarter flat year-on-year. While other regions showed relative resilience, these headwinds in Americas 2 and Capco offset some of the gains elsewhere and highlight regional and sector-specific fragilities.

One-Off Charges and Restructuring Cloud the Bottom Line

The quarter included sizable one-time P&L charges that reduced reported net income, even though they were excluded from IT Services segment margins. Wipro booked an INR 302 crore increase related to gratuity following a new labor code, along with around INR 263 crore in restructuring costs. Management indicated that the restructuring program is now effectively complete and that no additional related charges are expected, clearing the path for cleaner comparables in future quarters.

Acquisition-Related Dilution and Higher Amortization Ahead

Investors were reminded that acquisition-driven growth comes with near-term cost implications. Wipro flagged incremental margin dilution from the HARMAN DTS acquisition and noted accelerated amortization for a prior transaction during the quarter. Depreciation and amortization expenses will remain elevated and then normalize over time. Management nonetheless emphasized that the strategic benefits of enhanced engineering and AI capabilities are expected to outweigh the temporary drag on margins.

Large Deal Booking Volatility Driven by Timing, Not Competitiveness

Large deal bookings were softer this quarter compared with recent strong quarters, adding to concerns about the pace of growth. Management, however, framed this as a timing issue, with delays in some ramp-ups and typical lumpiness in the large-deal segment rather than a loss of competitiveness or structural demand weakness. The company’s focus remains on converting a solid pipeline into revenue as execution bottlenecks ease.

Guidance Signals Stability in Margins, Caution on Growth

Looking ahead, Wipro guided Q4 IT Services revenue to the $2.635–$2.688 billion range, implying flat to low-single-digit sequential growth in constant currency. The outlook factors in two months of contribution from HARMAN DTS but also fewer working days and delayed ramp-ups on some large contracts. Management aims to keep operating margins broadly within the 17%–17.5% band while absorbing acquisition-related dilution and managing future wage actions. With gross cash and investments around $6.5 billion and an interim dividend already declared, Wipro enters the next quarter with financial flexibility but tempered growth expectations.

In summary, Wipro’s latest earnings call painted a picture of a company executing well on margins, cash generation, and strategic repositioning around AI and engineering, but still grappling with muted demand, sector-specific pockets of weakness, and timing issues in large deals. For investors, the story is one of solid financial resilience and disciplined capital allocation against a backdrop of only modest near-term growth prospects, with the real upside hinging on how quickly the current pipeline converts into sustained revenue momentum.

data.title: Washington Federal Balances Growth, Credit Strains in Call
data.description:
Washington Federal ((WAFD)) has held its Q1 earnings call. Read on for the main highlights of the call.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Washington Federal’s latest earnings call struck a cautious but constructive tone, as management balanced strong bottom-line growth and capital returns with rising credit stress and near-term pressure on margins. Executives highlighted a 40% year-over-year jump in earnings per share and a leaner cost base, while openly acknowledging higher nonperforming assets, growing delinquencies and the earnings drag from nonaccrual loans and stepped-up securities purchases. The message to investors was clear: the bank is executing on its strategic pivot and capital deployment, but doing so against a more challenging credit and funding backdrop.

EPS Surges on Solid Quarter for Core Profitability

Washington Federal delivered net income available to common shareholders of $60.5 million, translating to diluted EPS of $0.79 for the quarter, up from $0.54 in the first quarter of fiscal 2025 and $0.72 in the prior quarter. Management emphasized that this represents 40% year-over-year EPS growth and 10% linked-quarter growth, underscoring improved profitability despite a flat reported net interest margin. The earnings beat was supported by stable net interest income, tighter expense control and a modest contribution from securities purchases, helping offset the impact of nonaccrual interest reversals and credit costs.

Loan Production Accelerates With a Growing, Diversified Pipeline

Loan growth remained a bright spot, with total originations of $1.1 billion outpacing $1.0 billion of repayments and payoffs. The production mix skewed toward business lending: 46% commercial and industrial (C&I), 23% commercial real estate, and 25% construction, consistent with management’s strategy to tilt the portfolio toward relationship-based business banking. The lending pipeline increased by $697 million, or 28% quarter-over-quarter, to $3.2 billion from $2.5 billion, giving management confidence in achieving its medium-term loan growth targets and signaling continuing demand across core markets.

Deposit Mix Improves Even as Overall Balances Edge Down

The bank reported notable progress in deposit composition, even though total deposits dipped by $21 million in the quarter. Noninterest-bearing deposits grew by $125 million, or 4.9% sequentially, and interest-bearing deposits rose $434 million, or 4.5%, while higher-cost time deposits fell by $580 million, down 6.4%. As a result, core deposits climbed to 79.7% of total deposits, up from 77.9% in the prior quarter, and the loan-to-deposit ratio improved to 92.7%, a significant shift from levels above 110% two years ago. Management acknowledged that deposit gathering remains competitive, but highlighted the mix shift toward lower-cost transaction accounts as a structural positive for funding.

Securities Purchases Add Incremental NII but Weigh on Near-Term Margin

Washington Federal leaned into its investment strategy this quarter, purchasing discount-priced agency mortgage-backed securities with an effective yield of 4.93%. Investment securities and MBS balances increased by $728 million, generating about $1.2 million of net interest income in the period and helping to offset runoff in the single-family mortgage book. While these purchases offer attractive risk-adjusted returns and diversify earning assets, management noted that their ramp-up contributes to near-term net interest margin pressure, as the yield benefit takes time to fully flow through.

Cost Discipline Drives Better Efficiency

Operating efficiency improved as management continued to moderate expenses. Total noninterest expense fell by $1.3 million, or 1.2% quarter-over-quarter, driven mainly by lower compensation and technology spending. This cost focus pushed the efficiency ratio down to 55.3%, from 56.8% in the prior quarter, aligning the bank more closely with its long-term operating goals. Management framed this as a key lever to protect earnings while navigating a tougher rate and credit environment, and a core component of its broader Build 2030 strategic plan.

Capital Strength Supports Ongoing Buybacks

The bank underscored its strong capital position, noting that all regulatory capital ratios remain comfortably above well-capitalized thresholds. Against this backdrop, Washington Federal continued to return capital to shareholders, repurchasing roughly 1.95 million shares at an average price of $29.75 during the quarter. These buybacks added an estimated $0.02 to EPS and leave authorization for approximately 6.3 million additional shares. Management pointed out that the stock trades around 1.1 times tangible book value, and reiterated its stance on opportunistic repurchases, signaling ongoing confidence in intrinsic value.

Wealth Management Launch Adds a New Fee-Income Leg

The launch of WaFd Wealth Management on August 31 marks a strategic step to diversify revenue beyond spread income. Assets under management have already grown to just over $400 million by quarter-end, and management is targeting $1 billion in AUM within two years. Early traction was described as encouraging, with the new platform expected to contribute to noninterest income growth over time and deepen client relationships, particularly in the bank’s expanding business and affluent customer segments.

Reserve Levels Bolstered to Cover Emerging Credit Risk

In response to rising credit stress indicators, Washington Federal increased its allowance for credit losses. The total allowance, including reserves for unfunded commitments, now stands at 1.05% of gross loans, up from 1.00% a year earlier. For commercial loans specifically, the allowance is 1.33% of net loans, compared with 1.26% a year ago. Management positioned these reserve levels as prudent given the recent migration of loans into criticized and nonperforming categories, aiming to provide investors with confidence that the balance sheet is appropriately buffered against potential losses.

Nonperforming Assets Jump, Pressuring Earnings Quality

Credit quality clearly moved in the wrong direction this quarter. Nonperforming assets rose to $203 million, or 0.75% of total assets, up from $143 million, or 0.54%, in the prior quarter. Nonaccrual loans alone increased by $62.7 million, a 49% jump since September 30, 2025. This spike not only reflects growing borrower stress but also weighs on reported results, as interest previously accrued on these loans must be reversed. Management acknowledged that these trends are a key watch item and a near-term drag on net interest margin.

Delinquencies and Criticized Loans Indicate Broader Credit Migration

Beyond headline nonperformers, early-stage credit metrics also deteriorated. Delinquent loans climbed to 1.07% of total loans, up sharply from 0.6% in the prior quarter and 0.3% a year ago. Criticized loans increased to 4.6% of net loans, from 4.39% in the prior quarter and 2.54% a year earlier, suggesting credit stress is broadening across multiple borrower segments rather than being confined to a handful of isolated issues. While absolute levels remain manageable by industry standards, the pace of deterioration will be critical for investors to track in upcoming quarters.

Charge-Offs Remain Low but Highlight Sector-Specific Weakness

Net charge-offs totaled $3.7 million for the quarter, equating to an annualized rate of roughly 7 basis points of total loans, still low in absolute terms. The quarter’s charge-offs were driven primarily by a single C&I energy relationship, where depressed oil prices and working capital constraints hurt the borrower’s ability to service debt. Management portrayed this as a contained, sector-specific issue rather than a systemic problem, but also as a reminder that certain cyclical industries are under pressure and warrant heightened monitoring.

Margin Squeezed by Nonaccruals and Securities Ramp-Up

Washington Federal’s reported net interest margin came in at 2.70%, essentially flat with the prior quarter’s 2.71%. However, management underscored that the underlying “spot” margin is closer to 2.79% absent unusual items. A key factor was approximately $5 million of interest reversals tied to loans moving to nonaccrual status, which created a roughly 9 basis-point gap between the spot margin and the reported figure. Additionally, accelerated MBS purchases shaved about 3 basis points from margin, as new securities take time to fully contribute to earnings. Management expects NIM to remain under pressure in the near term even as net interest income in dollar terms may continue to grow.

Funding Headwinds and Greater Reliance on Borrowings

Despite improvements in mix, total deposits were essentially flat to slightly down, decreasing by $21 million. To fund the sizable build in the securities portfolio, management increased borrowings by $671 million during the quarter, leading to a higher reliance on wholesale funding in the near term. Executives were candid that the deposit environment remains competitive and that growing low-cost relationship deposits is a work in progress. The current trajectory in core and noninterest-bearing deposits, however, is seen as laying the groundwork for a more durable funding base over time.

CRA Rating Uncertainty Adds a Regulatory Overhang

On the regulatory front, Washington Federal disclosed that it received a “Needs to Improve” Community Reinvestment Act rating from the FDIC and has appealed the determination. Management conceded that the appeal may not succeed and that the bank is preparing to move forward under the new rating. While this does not impact day-to-day operations, it can complicate branch relocation and expansion plans and may pose challenges for future mergers or acquisitions, introducing an additional strategic constraint investors will need to monitor.

Noninterest Income Healthy but Volatile

Noninterest income rose to $20.3 million, up $1.9 million from the prior quarter, but the composition was lumpy. Results benefited from a $3.2 million gain on the sale of a branch property, offset by a swing in equity method investment results, which produced a $408,000 loss this quarter compared with an $815,000 gain previously. This volatility underscores the importance of building more stable fee streams—such as the newly launched wealth management business—to complement earnings from balance sheet activities.

Forward Guidance: Margin Ambitions and Loan Growth Targets

Looking ahead, management’s guidance centered on rebuilding net interest margin and sustaining healthy loan growth while maintaining strong capital and liquidity. The bank aims to lift NIM to around 3.0% over the next two years, up from the current 2.70% reported margin and a 2.77% spot margin, with the yield on interest-earning assets at 5.05% and the cost of interest-bearing liabilities at 2.76%. Executives expect active loan growth of roughly 8–12% over the next one to two years, citing the $3.2 billion lending pipeline, and specifically pointed to a 6–10% growth range for fiscal 2026. On the funding side, Washington Federal plans to increase noninterest-bearing deposits to 20% of total deposits by 2030, from 12.6% today, building on recent growth in both balances and account counts. The bank targets an efficiency ratio near 55% and a higher return on tangible common equity—currently 10.6%, with management suggesting potential to approach the high teens if a 3% margin is achieved—while keeping the loan-to-deposit ratio in check and continuing opportunistic share repurchases under its remaining authorization.

In closing, Washington Federal’s earnings call painted a picture of a bank in transition: earnings are growing, costs are under control, capital is ample, and strategic initiatives like business banking expansion and wealth management are gaining traction. At the same time, investors must weigh mounting credit migration, higher nonperforming assets, and funding and margin headwinds that are unlikely to disappear quickly. For now, the story is one of balanced risks and rewards—solid execution and shareholder returns, tempered by a more demanding credit and regulatory environment that will define the next chapter for the franchise.

data.title: Options Volatility and Implied Earnings Moves This Week, January 20 – January 23, 2026
data.description:
This week, several major companies will be reporting earnings. Among them are Intel (INTC), Netflix (NFLX), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), GE Aerospace (GE), Procter & Gamble (PG).

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ahead of this week’s major earnings announcements, TipRanks has calculated the expected moves of these companies’ stocks. We use the at-the-money straddle of the options whose post-earnings expiration dates are closest to the earnings announcement date.

Note that options volatility tends to be high in the days leading up to an earnings announcement, due to uncertainty around the earnings. Investors don’t know whether the earnings will be positive or negative, so the price of options (aka the option premium) rises.

Furthermore, after the earnings announcement, because the uncertainty has been resolved, the price of options (the option premium) decreases substantially. This is known as the volatility crush.

Here are the major companies reporting this week and their expected earnings moves. Click on any ticker to see additional data about options on the stock, including real-time expected earnings moves, prices, volume, and open interest.

New insider activity at Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. ( (ACRV) ) has taken place on January 16, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc. has seen notable insider buying activity, signaling confidence from top leadership. Levy acquired 8,832 shares of the company’s stock, representing an additional investment of $14,926. These combined purchases underscore meaningful personal financial commitments by both the CEO and CFO to Acrivon Therapeutics, Inc.

Recent Updates on ACRV stock

In the last 24 hours, Acrivon Therapeutics (ACRV) has seen its outlook influenced mainly by a broad clinical and pipeline update and a follow-on R&D event, which together prompted at least one analyst to lift their price target. The key driver is stronger-than-expected Phase 2b data for lead asset ACR-368 in endometrial cancer, where updated interim results showed a 39% overall response rate in biomarker-positive monotherapy patients, higher response rates (44%) in less heavily pretreated patients, and particularly robust activity in serous endometrial cancer, including confirmed responses above 50% in certain subgroups. These data support a registrational-intent path, justify the strategic refocus and expansion of the serous endometrial cancer arm across European sites, and underpin plans for a Phase 3 confirmatory trial combining ACR-368 with anti–PD-1 therapy, all of which improve perceived probability of clinical and regulatory success. Additional upside to sentiment comes from early but favorable Phase 1 data for ACR-2316, which showed acceptable tolerability and tumor shrinkage (including partial responses in multiple AP3-prioritized tumor types), and from the nomination of ACR-6840, a potential first-in-class CDK11 inhibitor with an IND targeted for late 2026, reinforcing the depth and sustainability of the AP3-driven precision oncology pipeline. While technical indicators are currently constructive and the company maintains a low-debt balance sheet, some analysts remain cautious due to the absence of revenue, ongoing cash burn, and long-dated clinical risk, tempering enthusiasm despite the positive trial updates and contributing to a more balanced overall rating profile.

Spark’s Take on ACRV Stock

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance—no revenue, large and persistent losses, and substantial cash burn—despite low leverage. data.title: Massive Insider Move: Major Investor Makes Colossal Bet on Mainz Biomed
data.description:
New insider activity at Mainz Biomed B.V. ( (MYNZ) ) has taken place on January 16, 2026.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

Significant shareholder Kevin Michael Donahue has dramatically increased his stake in Mainz Biomed B.V. by purchasing 643,850 shares of the company’s stock. The total value of this transaction amounts to an astonishing $258,827,700,000, signaling an extremely large capital commitment and a strong show of confidence in Mainz Biomed B.V.’s future prospects.

Recent Updates on MYNZ stock

In the last 24 hours, coverage of Mainz Biomed B.V. (MYNZ) has focused on the company’s review of its 2025 accomplishments and its development roadmap, rather than on immediate price action or explicit analyst target revisions. Management highlighted progress in its eAArly DETECT 2 feasibility study for colorectal cancer screening and ongoing work on a blood-based test for early pancreatic cancer detection, emphasizing these programs as key drivers of its longer-term growth strategy and future pivotal FDA study (ReconAAsense) planned for 2026. The broader context from recent company moves—including a $150M mixed securities shelf filing, amendments to boost share capital, and commercial expansion of its ColoAlert test via platforms like DoctorBox and through a partnership to bring ColoAlert to South America—provides the fundamental backdrop that could inform any analyst reassessment of valuation, particularly around expectations for capital needs, regulatory milestones, and market expansion, even though specific price changes or recommendations are not disclosed in the provided text.

Spark’s Take on MYNZ Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MYNZ is a Neutral.

Overall score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing losses, high leverage, and negative operating cash flow with reliance on financing). Technical indicators are moderately supportive in the near term (above 20D/50D with neutral momentum), but the longer-term trend remains negative. Valuation impact is neutral because P/E and dividend yield are unavailable.
